name: Bongwoori
description: ArcLight-derived voltage indicator with an improved voltage-sensing domain.

spectrum.name: Bongwoori
spectrum.note: Fluorophore is Super Ecliptic pHluorin (A227D), the GFP-derived reporter shared across the ArcLight/Bongwoori family. Super ecliptic pHluorin carries the S65T (EGFP) mutation, so it is spectrally near-identical to EGFP and no separate measured curve is published; the displayed curve is the FPbase EGFP excitation + emission verbatim (peaks 490/511). Previously the emission was the EGFP curve red-shifted +4 nm (a fabricated shift that placed the peak at 515 nm); the shift has been removed (minEm 476→472). Same fix as the Marina spectrum.

voltage.name: Bongwoori
voltage.note: Read directly (signed) from the Lee 2017 Fig. 4B bar graph, which plots -ΔF/F (%) for Bongwoori at the exact 100-mV-step voltages against a -70 mV holding potential (legend: -120, -20, 30, 80, 130 mV; 34°C, HEK293, 1P). Bongwoori (leftmost group) reads -ΔF/F ≈ 0 (-120), 4.3 (-20), 13.4 (30), 18.5 (80), 19.3 (130) %, i.e. signed ΔF/F ≈ +1, -4, -13, -19, -19 with -70 mV = 0. Negative-going, monotonic, saturates near -19% by +80 mV; the -70→+30 mV step is -13% (matches dynamicRangeData). Previous curve was digitized from the Piao 2015 Fig. 6B NORMALIZED Boltzmann (0→1) and rescaled to a -10% anchor, which understated the response (had +30 = -10, +80 = -13); the -10% anchor was actually the ArcLight scale bar from Fig. 7A, not a Bongwoori 100-mV value. Lee Fig. 4B is a direct signed reading at the target steps, so it supersedes the rescaled Boltzmann. sourceImage updated to the Fig. 4B crop (was the Piao Fig. 6B normalized plot).

kinetics.note: Weighted (amplitude-weighted) τ from the bi-exponential fit, Piao 2015 Table 2: τon = 10 ± 1 ms, τoff = 7 ± 1 ms (for a 100-mV depolarization, HEK293). The on-response fast component alone is 8 ± 1 ms (91% amplitude); previously τon was stored as that fast component (8) rather than the weighted mean (10).

Weighted τ from the bi-exponential fit, Lee 2017 Table 2: τon = 17 ± 1 ms (fast 9 ms 76%, slow 40 ms), τoff = 14 ± 1 ms (fast 8 ms 86%, slow 52 ms). Previously τon/τoff were stored as the fast components (9 / 8) rather than the weighted means (17 / 14).

dynamicRangeData.note: ΔF/F for the -70→+30 mV (100-mV) step read directly off the Lee 2017 Fig. 4B bar graph: Bongwoori -ΔF/F ≈ 13.4% at +30 mV (holding -70 mV), 34°C, HEK293, 1P widefield. Was previously -10% cited to Piao 2015 'Fig. 6A' — but the -10% figure was the ArcLight (not Bongwoori) scale bar in Fig. 7A neuron traces, a misattribution; Fig. 4B gives the correct signed 100-mV value. Consistent with the F-V curve (voltage.custom).

sensitivityData.note: Estimated per-AP ΔF/F from the Piao 2015 Fig. 7A Bongwoori single-trial neuron trace (hippocampal neuron, current clamp, 1P widefield); the panel shows a -4% ΔF/F scale bar and the filtered spike deflections are roughly half that (~2-3%). No numeric per-AP value is printed in Piao 2015 (only the scale bar), so this is an approximate read, not a stated value. The Lee 2017 Table 3 entry (3.7%) is the well-defined, tabulated per-spike ΔF/F and is preferred.

photobleach.note: Cross-GEVI value from the Bongwoori-R3 origin paper (Lee et al. 2017, primary subject Bongwoori-R3). Green ■ Bongwoori curve — bleaches faster initially than Bongwoori-R3 but levels off, retaining ~23% at 1540 s (slightly above ArcLight A242's ~18%). Re-digitized 2026-07-02 (color-masked green squares, axis-calibrated 6 y-ticks 1.0→0.0 rows 172→1392 / 4 x-ticks 0→1500 cols 607→2510, stretched-exponential fit r²=0.995, trace + fit + t75 overlay-verified against Fig. S1); the prior trace bleached too fast (t75 100 s, tail 0.187 = ArcLight-orange level) — corrected to t75 ≈ 124 s (stretched-exp fit; direct F=0.75 crossing of the digitized points ≈ 126 s). Origin-paper photobleach Fig. S1 (HEK293 cells, Xenon arc lamp, repeating 40 s on / 20 s off, measured every 40 s, 1 mW/mm² 1P). All three GEVIs (ArcLight A242, Bongwoori, Bongwoori-R3) were measured under IDENTICAL conditions, so the cross-overlay (shared source+Fig. S1) is genuinely iso-condition. The paper reports the exponential photobleach τ is >450 s for all three but gives no exact per-GEVI value. intensityMWmm2 is omitted: 1 mW/mm² is 100× below the 100 mW/mm² reference, so linear dose-scaling would give a misleadingly tiny t75 — the value stands at native 1 mW/mm².
